overview: Grixis Affinity is a value-focused control deck in Pauper that relies on card advantage, efficient removal, and resilient creatures to outlast opponents. Despite a slower early game due to tap lands, it excels at trading at instant speed and overwhelming opponents with accumulated resources. Key synergies like Krark-Clan Shaman and Toxin Analysis provide board control, while Refurbished Familiar offers consistent pressure and card selection.
win_conditions: Drowning opponent in resources to control the game
Krark-Clan Shaman and Toxin Analysis combo
Refurbished Familiar pressure and card advantage

keep_criteria: Hands that draw cards efficiently on the first few turns
Hands that develop the game plan
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one mana accelerator or cheap artifact, and a turn-1/2 play (Arcbound Ravager, Myr Enforcer, or Thoughtcast paired with an artifact).
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early artifacts, or hands with only removal/draw spells and no way to cast them on curve.

examples.reason: Multiple lands and early card draw/removal align with prioritizing efficient first-turn development.

early_game: Play slowly due to tap lands
Prioritize efficient card draw and game plan development
Turn 1: Play a land, then a cheap artifact or mana rock.
If you have a cantrip, cast it to dig.
Turn 2: Deploy an Arcbound Ravager or Myr Enforcer, using artifacts for mana or +1/+1 counters.
Sequence lands to ensure you can hit your colors for Thoughtcast or Galvanic Blast if needed, but prioritize artifact acceleration.
mid_game: Disrupt opponent's board with Krark-Clan Shaman and Toxin Analysis
Use Thoughtcast to cycle cheap artifacts and draw spells, keeping hand size stable.
Don't overcommit mana to artifacts if you're low on interaction.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the opponent has no answers.
Hold back if they have sweepers or targeted removal.
late_game: Take full control of the game after opponent spends resources
A fully powered Arcbound Ravager or Myr Enforcer, often buffed by artifacts or combat tricks.
Reckoner's Bargain can also close games by drawing you into a win condition or enabling a graveyard combo.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to lethal, the opponent has no sweepers, or you've established card advantage that they can't match.

key_cards.name: Refurbished Familiar
key_cards.notes: 2/1 flying that enters for {1}{U}, removes resources or gives card advantage.

key_cards.name: Krark-Clan Shaman
key_cards.notes: Creature-shaped removal that destroys nonflying creatures and combos with Toxin Analysis.

key_cards.name: Cast Down
key_cards.notes: Essential removal for threats Krark or Galvanic Blast can't reach.

key_cards.name: Galvanic Blast
key_cards.notes: Nearly always deals 4 damage; can be held in hand until opponent is in range then played all at once.

key_cards.name: Candy Trail
key_cards.notes: Lets you select cards and provides card advantage as well as life gain.

key_cards.name: Myr Enforcer
key_cards.notes: Extremely effective on battlefield and excellent sacrifice target for Reckoner's Bargain.

sequencing_tips: Keep two or three Galvanic Blasts in hand until opponent is in range, then play them all at once
Trade well at instant speed to punish opponent mistakes
Sacrifice Myr Enforcer to Reckoner's Bargain when facing Kuldotha Red
Use Krark-Clan Shaman and Toxin Analysis to clear the board after disrupting ramp
Common mistake: Overcommitting to artifacts without interaction, leaving you vulnerable to sweepers.
Unique interaction: Thoughtcast can be used to cycle artifacts into the graveyard for Reckoner's Bargain or other synergies.
if they have no cards, they're likely on a combo or control plan.

matchups.advice: Stick Myr Enforcers and Refurbished Familiars for pressure, use Krark+Toxin but watch for protection.
Play reactively, trade at instant speed, and side in Red Elemental Blast.
Disrupt ramp game plan, use Krark+Toxin to clear board, and take control after resources are spent.
Don't worry too much about graveyard, avoid drowning in 1/1 tokens.
Protect life total, sacrifice Myr Enforcer to Reckoner's Bargain, and side in Blue Elemental Blast.

Use Thoughtcast to dig for multiple spells, forcing them to counter multiple threats. Cast artifacts and spells at unexpected times (end step, after they tap out). Use Galvanic Blast to remove their key counterspell sources if possible.
Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use Galvanic Blast to remove their ramp sources or early threats. Don't let them stabilize; keep the board state volatile.
Prioritize removal and life total. Use artifacts as blockers early. Reckoner's Bargain can stabilize your hand. Don't overextend into combat tricks.
Pressure their life total while they dig for answers. Use Thoughtcast to out-draw them. Force them to use interaction on low-impact artifacts to set up bigger threats later.
Win the resource war. Prioritize Thoughtcast and card draw to out-value them. Use discard to strip their key artifacts. Protect your own engine with counters or protection.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, targeted removal, and combo win conditions.
Note when they tap out for a single spell—that's your window to attack or cast Thoughtcast.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Currently, Affinity plays like a control deck with a game plan that focuses very heavily on value.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: drown your opponent in them and control the game
the combo with Toxin Analysis is simply absurd
Refurbished Familiar is one of the main reasons why this deck is still relevant

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: keep two or three Galvanics in your hand until your opponent is just in range of them, and then play all of them at once
Affinity can play reactive matches really well: it trades well at instant speed
Myr Enforcer is an excellent sacrifice for Reckoner's Bargain
use Krark + Toxin to clear the board

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Myr Enforcer is an excellent sacrifice for Reckoner's Bargain
when they don't have counters, Affinity is favored
be careful with your opponent's protection
